commandsRun: FOR UPDATE single-tx refactor of campaign-runner
implReport.e2eSummary: Playwright editor e2e 2 passed; LocalStack image pipeline e2e 2 passed.
implReport.notes: ROOT CAUSE (Phase-10 flake): campaign-runner claim and advance were in SEPARATE transactions; between a run's claim-commit and advance-commit the row sat active at the claim's updated_at, so a second run whose SELECT landed in that window claimed it too, and the loser's guarded advance updated 0 rows but runEnrollment never checked the rowcount -> two ticks returned 'completed'. Raw CAS SQL itself was correct (20/20 single-winner probe). FIX: FOR UPDATE single-tx — added withTx/withWorkspaceTx (one connection: BEGIN; SELECT...FOR UPDATE the enrollment + re-check status=active inside the lock; all reads+writes+advance on that client; COMMIT; enqueue {outbox_id} after commit). A concurrent run blocks on FOR UPDATE then sees status!=active and skips -> exactly one completed. Legacy CAS path retained for in-memory unit tests; runStatementsInWorkspaceTx kept for enroll.ts. SEPARATE issue exposed: starting LocalStack (for Phase 11 images) un-skipped services/processor/test/fifo-localstack.e2e.test.ts because it was gated on LOCALSTACK_URL ?? AWS_ENDPOINT_URL; under vitest's module loader the AWS SDK SQS HTTP path fails with QueueDoesNotExist (works identically in plain Node — verified via standalone probe round-tripping a real FIFO message via the exact buildSqsMessage path). Restored its documented LOCALSTACK_URL-only opt-in gate + documented the harness limitation (wiring is proven, not a product bug). Also added AWS_ENDPOINT_URL to turbo.json test env passthrough.
implReport.testSummary: Fixed the Phase-10 concurrency flake + a newly-exposed LocalStack gating issue. Full cold suite 36/36 turbo tasks, 0 cached. campaign-runner 65/65; idempotent-concurrent-sweep now deterministic (13/13 standalone across agent+orchestrator). typecheck + lint OK. Web Playwright e2e 2 passed. No regression.

invariants: Tenant isolation: every tenant-scoped row carries workspace_id NOT NULL; every query filters by workspace_id. Service-role Lambdas bypass RLS so they MUST scope by workspace_id in code; the admin app uses the user JWT so RLS applies.

workspace_id is NEVER from a client payload — it comes from the API-key/authorizer context or a trusted server-set field. The frontend sends the active-workspace claim via its JWT; the API resolves scope from the authorizer, never from a body field.

Role enforcement (§3A): owner/marketer/accounting are workspace-scoped; system-admin is the only cross-tenant role and every cross-tenant access is audit-logged. The UI shows only what the role permits; the API enforces it regardless of the UI.

All sends (broadcasts §9A and campaign actions §9B) go through the SAME outbox -> Dispatcher pipeline (§9) and pass suppression -> frequency-cap -> quiet-hours; sending is gated on workspace active/verified.

Idempotency: broadcasts dedupe per (broadcast_id, profile_id); the campaign runner must tolerate retries without double-advancing (optimistic checks / updated_at); image uploads + metering rollups are idempotent.

LOCAL-only: AWS (SES/SNS/S3/SQS) are MOCKED (aws-sdk-client-mock) or run on LocalStack; never hit real AWS. Do NOT mock Postgres in the integration tier — use the real local DB on localhost:5433. The editor must emit MJML, never hand-rolled email HTML.

Logic in pure injected functions; Lambda handlers stay thin. Reuse the prior harness (packages/db testutil: adminPool, hasDatabaseUrl, applyMigrations; runPlanInWorkspaceTx; SqlStatement {text,values}; @cdp/segments resolveAudience; @cdp/email; the dispatcher core).

Determinism: turbo test task is cache:false — keep it. Unique workspace UUIDs + file-local namespaces per integration file (events.event_id / ses_message_id are GLOBAL keys). No cross-file races. Frontend e2e (Playwright) must be deterministic (seeded data, stable selectors).
Scope discipline: implement exactly what the cited sections require for THIS phase — no features beyond the spec. Keep the cost model EXACTLY as §20 (direct usage + even split of fixed costs).
context.loop: This phase has a FRONTEND/UI surface. The widest quality loop is a REAL BROWSER: build the UI with Vite + Preact (or React) and verify with Playwright e2e against a locally-running app + local API (thin adapter/serverless-offline over the Lambda handlers + LocalStack). Use the core GrapesJS + MJML plugin (BSD-3, free) — never the paid Studio SDK; the editor must emit MJML. Seed deterministic data and use stable selectors so the e2e is reliable.

criteria: The Image Lambda issues a presigned S3 PUT URL under the workspace_id/ key prefix; uploaded image bytes are recorded in usage_counters; variants are produced by the sharp step. (Verified against LocalStack S3 / mocked S3.)
The GrapesJS+MJML editor renders in a real browser (Playwright), lets a user compose a template, and on save emits MJML that compiles to HTML stored in email_templates (workspace-scoped).
Image keys are workspace-prefixed; one workspace cannot read/overwrite another workspace prefix.
scope: services/image: an Image Lambda that returns a presigned S3 PUT URL keyed under a workspace_id/ prefix (LocalStack S3); an S3-triggered Lambda that makes sharp variants; record bytes in usage_counters (image_storage_bytes). Mock/LocalStack S3.

Frontend (in /web): a GrapesJS + MJML-plugin email editor component (core GrapesJS, BSD-3, NOT the paid Studio SDK) that emits MJML; on save, MJML -> compiled HTML via the existing Template Lambda (@cdp/email compile) stored in email_templates. Images uploaded via the presigned URL flow.
The editor must OUTPUT MJML, not hand-rolled email HTML.
phase.specSections: §11 (presigned S3 PUT under workspace_id/ prefix, sharp variants via S3-triggered Lambda, CloudFront, GrapesJS+MJML editor emitting MJML), §6 (usage_counters image bytes)
phase.title: Image pipeline + WYSIWYG editor

e2eFlows: LocalStack S3: presign PUT (ws A) -> PUT bytes -> HeadObject A/ -> synthetic ObjectCreated -> variant Lambda sharp variants under A/ -> usage_counters bytes in real PG
Cross-workspace: presigned A URL rejected for B/ key; variant handler refuses prefix mismatch
Browser editor: Vite boot -> GrapesJS+MJML mounts -> compose -> Save -> API compileMjml + buildTemplateUpsert -> email_templates has editor MJML + server HTML, workspace-scoped
implUnits: BOOTSTRAP /web Vite + Preact (index.html, vite.config @preact/preset-vite, main.tsx, scripts)
web EmailEditor Preact component embedding core grapesjs (BSD-3) + grapesjs-mjml plugin (NOT Studio SDK) emitting MJML
web pure serializeEditorToMjml + buildSaveTemplatePayload (+ <mj-image src>)
web save action (POST {name,mjml}) + image upload action (presign -> PUT -> insert CloudFront URL)
services/image: s3-key.ts, presign.ts, variants.ts, usage.ts
services/image presign handler thin (injected S3Client+presigner+authorizer ctx)
services/image S3-triggered variant handler thin (sharp + S3 get/put + buildImageBytesUpsert scoped by key prefix)
save-template endpoint (compileMjml + buildTemplateUpsert)
deps/config bucket/CloudFront base/presign TTL/contentType whitelist via @cdp/shared

tooling: @aws-sdk/client-s3, @aws-sdk/s3-request-presigner, sharp (image); grapesjs, grapesjs-mjml, preact, vite, @preact/preset-vite (web); @playwright/test; playwright.config webServer (vite preview) + DATABASE_URL
Playwright install: pnpm --filter @cdp/web exec playwright install --with-deps chromium; e2e gated skip when no browser/DB
mocks: aws-sdk-client-mock S3Client for presign-handler integration (real getSignedUrl drives URL shape)
LocalStack S3 (docker-compose, localhost:4566, forcePathStyle) for e2e upload + variant flow
REAL Postgres localhost:5433 for usage_counters + email_templates (never mocked)
REAL browser via Playwright/Chromium for editor e2e
Playwright network interception to assert save body mjml
injected authorizer/workspace context (workspace never from body)
plan.notes: Order: pure units -> integration real PG (record-usage, save-template reuse buildTemplateUpsert/compileMjml) -> S3 aws-sdk-client-mock presign handler -> thin LocalStack e2e upload+variant+usage -> BOOTSTRAP Vite+Preact web -> GrapesJS+MJML editor -> Playwright browser e2e last. Invariants: workspace_id never client-supplied (authorizer context/API key); every S3 key under ${workspace_id}/; assertKeyInWorkspace guards service-role variant Lambda (bypasses RLS); buildImageBytesUpsert binds workspace_id $1. Editor MUST emit MJML (asserted unit+integration+browser); compiled HTML server-side via compileMjml at save, stored alongside mjml, never browser-authored. LocalStack S3 via pnpm localstack:start; real PG localhost:5433. e2e/integration guarded describe.skipIf so unit CI stays green without Docker/DB/Chromium. sharp real in LocalStack tier (real Node runtime). CloudFront/IAM/S3-trigger wiring is CDK (infra), asserted only at thin e2e wiring level.

assertions: buildImageKey prefixes ${workspaceId}/
stable per-upload segment (no overwrite)
throws falsy workspaceId
filename sanitized (.. / separators stripped, can't escape prefix)
parseWorkspaceFromKey + assertKeyInWorkspace throws on prefix mismatch
buildPresignRequest PutObjectCommand Key starts ${workspaceId}/
contentType whitelist png/jpeg/webp/gif, reject others
workspace from context arg never body
presignPutUrl returns {url,key,expiresIn} short TTL
planVariants canonical set under same workspace prefix
never upscale (skip width>=source)
names sharp resize op pure no invocation
throws if source key prefix unparseable
buildImageBytesUpsert workspace_id $1, usage_counters metric=image_storage_bytes
additive upsert ON CONFLICT (workspace_id,period,metric) DO UPDATE value=value+EXCLUDED
period normalized first-of-month
throws falsy workspaceId/negative bytes
aws-sdk-client-mock S3Client + real getSignedUrl -> presigned PUT URL path includes ${workspaceId}/
workspaceId from authorizer context not body
no-workspace -> 4xx no presign
disallowed contentType -> 4xx no S3
LocalStack S3: presigned PUT for ws A uploads to A/<key> (HeadObject confirms + ContentLength)
synthetic S3 ObjectCreated event -> variant Lambda produces sharp variants under A/ (HeadObject each, bytes>0, format/width per spec)
source bytes recorded usage_counters image_storage_bytes for A (real PG)
cross-workspace: presigned A URL rejected for B/ key; variant handler refuses event whose prefix != deriving workspace (assertKeyInWorkspace)
buildImageBytesUpsert real PG inserts usage_counters row
second upsert ADDS to value (additive accumulation)
ws B doesn't alter ws A counter
serializeEditorToMjml root <mjml> with mj-body, NEVER hand-rolled html/table
output valid for compileMjml (non-empty, no throw)
image -> <mj-image src=cloudfront-url> not data: URI
buildSaveTemplatePayload {name,mjml} only, no compiled HTML (server-side at save)
save path compileMjml then buildTemplateUpsert real PG -> one email_templates row mjml + compiled_html scoped workspaceId
re-save same name UPDATEs in place recompiled
same name ws B separate row, A untouched
stored compiled_html == compileMjml(stored mjml)
Playwright Chromium: GrapesJS+MJML editor mounts (canvas iframe + MJML blocks), free BSD-3 core no Studio paywall
compose template (drag block, type) + Save
Save POST body mjml starts <mjml> (intercepted)
after save email_templates row (real PG) non-empty mjml + compiled_html parses as HTML
row scoped to test workspace_id from auth context not client-sent

instructions: Clear the turbo cache and RUN the tests yourself (DATABASE_URL set) twice from cold, plus pnpm typecheck and pnpm lint. For UI phases, RUN the Playwright e2e in a real browser. Record real pass/fail. Confirm the integration tier RUNS (not skipped) and no regression in earlier phases.

Verify EACH acceptance criterion is covered by a passing, non-vacuous test: ["The Image Lambda issues a presigned S3 PUT URL under the workspace_id/ key prefix; uploaded image bytes are recorded in usage_counters; variants are produced by the sharp step. (Verified against LocalStack S3 / mocked S3.)","The GrapesJS+MJML editor renders in a real browser (Playwright), lets a user compose a template, and on save emits MJML that compiles to HTML stored in email_templates (workspace-scoped).","Image keys are workspace-prefixed; one workspace cannot read/overwrite another workspace prefix."].

Highest-priority checks: (a) tenant isolation + role enforcement (server-side, not just UI-hidden); (b) all sends route through the Dispatcher guards; (c) idempotency (broadcast dedupe / no double-advance / idempotent rollups); (d) AWS mocked/LocalStack (no real AWS) yet asserted; (e) for UI, the flow actually works in a real browser against the real backend, not a stub.
If any integration test mocks Postgres, that is a FAIL. If a UI criterion is only unit-tested (no real browser), that is a FAIL.
Scope check: flag any feature beyond the cited sections; verify the §20 cost model is exact.
Score 0-100. Set testsPass and criteriaAllMet honestly. Give concrete prioritized recommendations.
prompt.outputFormat: JSON: { score: number, testsPass: boolean, criteriaAllMet: boolean, criteriaCoverage: [{criterion, met, evidence}], isolationOk: boolean, browserVerified: boolean, scopeCreep: string[], recommendations: string[], criticalIssues: string[] }
prompt.role: adversarial principal reviewer + QA engineer verifying spec-fidelity, tenant isolation, roles, and (for UI) real-browser behavior
prompt.task: Independently verify "Image pipeline + WYSIWYG editor" against its §18 criteria. Re-run the tests yourself (cold cache); do not trust the self-report.

title: Quality gate: Image pipeline + WYSIWYG editor (iteration 2)
